subject: UX & Research
title: WCAG and Web Standards
description: A quick win: understand WCAG and Web Standards.

hook.text: WCAG: four letters that decide if a billion people can use your site. Web Content Accessibility Guidelines.

buildup.text: WCAG 1.0 arrived in 1999. Version 2.0 in 2008 introduced testable success criteria at three levels.

discovery.text: POUR: Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, Robust. Four principles for accessible web content.

twist.text: Most sites claim AA compliance. Few actually test it. Automated scans catch only 30% of real issues.

climax.text: WCAG 2.2 added rules for mobile, touch targets, and focus. Standards evolve as technology does.

punchline.text: Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, Robust. Four pillars of web access.

quiz.question: What are the four WCAG principles (POUR)?
